heading: Introduction
text: Welcome SequiaViz documentation…

heading: How to install and run
text: install run SequiaViz, follow steps:Install required R packages.Set directory structure.Upload data files.

heading: Input file types and formats
text: SequiaViz integrates results four types genomic datasets:

heading: 1. Somatic Variant Calling
text:
Exploration visualization somatic mutations tumor samplesRequired files:
.tsv | .vcf file annotated variants (.vcf implemented yet)
.tsv | .vcf file annotated variants (.vcf implemented yet)Optional files (needed IGV visualization):
Tumor DNA .bam + .bai files
Normal DNA .bam + .bai files
Tumor mutational burden (TMB) .tsv | .xlsx | .txt file
Tumor DNA .bam + .bai filesNormal DNA .bam + .bai filesTumor mutational burden (TMB) .tsv | .xlsx | .txt file

heading: 2. Germline Variant Calling
text:
Exploration visualization germline mutations normal samplesRequired files:
.tsv | .vcf file annotated variants (.vcf implemented yet)
.tsv | .vcf file annotated variants (.vcf implemented yet)Optional files (needed IGV visualization):
Normal .bam + .bai files
Normal .bam + .bai files

heading: 3. Fusion Genes Detection
text:
Exploration visualization gene fusions RNA-seq dataRequired files:
.tsv | .xlsx files detected genes fusion (.tsv implemented yet)
.tsv | .xlsx files detected genes fusion (.tsv implemented yet)Optional files (needed IGV visualization row expansion preview):
Tumor RNA .bam + .bai files
Chimeric .bam + .bai files
Arriba .pdf + .tsv files (embedded report display quality metrics)
Tumor RNA .bam + .bai filesChimeric .bam + .bai filesArriba .pdf + .tsv files (embedded report display quality metrics)

heading: 4. Expression Profile
text:
Exploration visualization gene expression different tissuesRequired files:
.tsv | .xlsx | .txt file expression data sample files tissue-matched reference expression data
.tsv | .xlsx | .txt file expression data sample files tissue-matched reference expression dataOptional files:
Genes interest (GOI) .tsv | .xlsx | .txt file
Genes interest (GOI) .tsv | .xlsx | .txt file

heading: Recommended Directory Structure
text: … case multiple tissue-matched reference expression data:
code:
project_root/
├── somatic_data/
│   ├── mutation_loads.tsv                    # tumor mutational burden table
│   ├── patient_001/
│   │   ├── variants.tsv                      # somatic variants table
│   │   ├── tumor.bam                         # tumor DNA BAM
│   │   ├── tumor.bam.bai                     # index for tumor DNA BAM
│   │   ├── normal.bam                        # normal DNA BAM
│   │   └── normal.bam.bai                    # index for normal DNA BAM
│   ├── patient_002/
│   │   └── ... (same structure)
│   └── patient_N/
│       └── ... (same structure)
├── germline_data/
│   ├── patient_001/
│   │   ├── variants.tsv                      # germline variants table
│   │   ├── normal.bam                        # normal DNA BAM
│   │   └── normal.bam.bai                    # index for normal DNA BAM
│   ├── patient_002/
│   │   └── ... (same structure)
│   └── patient_N/
│       └── ... (same structure)
├── fusion_data/
│   ├── patient_001/
│   │   ├── fusions.tsv                       # detected gene fusions table
│   │   ├── fusion.bam                        # tumor RNA BAM
│   │   ├── fusion.bam.bai                    # index for tumor RNA BAM
│   │   ├── chimeric.bam                      # chimeric BAM
│   │   ├── chimeric.bam.bai                  # index for chimeric BAM
│   │   ├── arriba_report.pdf                 # arriba report
│   │   └── arriba_results.tsv                # arriba result table
│   ├── patient_002/
│   │   └── ... (same structure)
│   └── patient_N/
│       └── ... (same structure)                  
└── expression_data/
    ├── genes_of_interest.tsv                 # table of genes of interest
    ├── patient_001/
    │   └── expression.tsv                    # gene expression table
    ├── patient_002/
    │   └── ... (same structure)
    └── patient_N/
        └── ... (same structure)   └── expression_data/
    ├── genes_of_interest.tsv                 # list of genes of interest
    ├── patient_001/
    ├── blood_expression.tsv                  # expression in blood
    │   └── liver_expression.tsv              # expression in liver
    ├── patient_002/
    │   └── ... (same structure)
    └── patient_N/
        └── ... (same structure)

heading: General Rules
text: Use consistent naming across patientsAvoid special characters spacesUse underscores dots separatorsAll file names must unique within directoryAll per-patient files must patient ID dataset type (somatic, germline, fusion, expression|RNAseq) somewhere path nameExamples:Note: GOI TMB files considered per-patient files contain information samples

heading: 1 BAM File Patterns
text: BAM files optional needed IGV visualization. Define patterns different types:

heading: 1.1 Patterns for Somatic Variant Calling
text: Tumor pattern: “tumor”, “FFPE”, “tkane”Normal pattern: “normal”, “control”, “krev”

heading: 1.2 Patterns for Germline Variant Calling
text: Normal pattern: “normal”, “control”, “krev”

heading: 1.3 Patterns for Fusion Detection
text: Tumor pattern: “tumor”, “fuze”, “RNA”Chimeric pattern: “chimeric”, “Chimeric.”

heading: 2.1 Structure for Patient “DZ1601”
code:
DZ1601/
├── DZ1601.variants.tsv                 # somatic variants
├── DZ1601_germline.variants.tsv         # germline variants  
├── DZ1601.xlsx                         # gene fusions
├── DZ1601_tumor.bam                    # tumor BAM
├── DZ1601_tumor.bam.bai                # tumor BAM index
├── DZ1601_normal.bam                   # normal BAM
├── DZ1601_normal.bam.bai               # normal BAM index
├── DZ1601_chimeric.bam                 # chimeric BAM
├── DZ1601_chimeric.bam.bai             # chimeric BAM index
├── DZ1601_arriba.pdf                   # Arriba report
├── DZ1601_arriba.tsv                   # Arriba results
├── DZ1601_Blood.tsv                    # expression in blood
├── DZ1601_Liver.tsv                    # expression in liver
└── DZ1601_genes_of_interest.tsv        # genes of interest

heading: 2.2 Example variants.tsv
code:
var_name    sample  gene    chromosome  position    ref alt in_library
chr1:12345:A>G  DZ1601  BRCA1   1   12345   A   G   2/10
chr2:67890:C>T  DZ1601  TP53    2   67890   C   T   1/10

heading: 2.3 Example expression.tsv
code:
gene_name   sample  tissue  expression_value    pathway
BRCA1   DZ1601  Blood   12.5    DNA_repair
TP53    DZ1601  Blood   8.3 p53_pathway
BRCA1   DZ1601  Liver   15.2    DNA_repair

heading: 3.1 Before Upload
text: Check data completeness - make sure required filesVerify naming - patient ID must consistent across filesGenerate BAI indexes BAM filesCheck format - TSV files must tabs separators

heading: 3.2 Performance Optimization
text: File size: Recommended maximum BAM file size: 2 GBCompression: Use compressed formats possibleNetwork location: large files consider local storage

heading: 3.3 Backup and Versioning
text: Backup original data modificationsUse versioning track changes dataDocument changes data future reference

heading: Upload data
text: module serves starting point SequiaViz application. uploading data, ensure input files directories properly formatted according specifications outlined section Data requirements .

Select main directory containing data patients.
example bellow, main directory project_root, contains subdirectories patient (patient_001, …, patient_N) general files like genes_of_interest.tsv mutation_loads.tsv.

Set patterns BAM files (e.g., “tumor”, “FFPE”, “normal”, “ctrl”, “chimeric”). pattern set, BAM BAI files searched .

heading: Step 2: Validation and confirmation
text:
application searches selected directory identifies relevant files.displays overview files found patient selected dataset.marks problems using colored icons:
🟢 Green: required files found (e.g., variant file, fusion file, expression file).
🟠 Orange: Optional files missing (e.g., BAM files IGV visualization). can continue without files, features unavailable.
🔴 Red: Required files missing many files. must resolve issues proceeding. See Common Errors Solutions section help.
🟢 Green: required files found (e.g., variant file, fusion file, expression file).🟠 Orange: Optional files missing (e.g., BAM files IGV visualization). can continue without files, features unavailable.🔴 Red: Required files missing many files. must resolve issues proceeding. See Common Errors Solutions section help.Confirm configuration continueNote: realize Step 2 made mistake Step 1, can go back Step 1 using Back Selection button adjust configuration. didn’t make mistake notice files missing, can add directory rename needed. , use Refresh button search files .Note2: previously worked configuration saved session, can upload directly using Load Session button. allow application skip Selection Validation steps load configuration saved file.

heading: 3.3.1 “Required file missing”
text:
Cause: Required file selected analysis type missingSolution:
- Check file exists patient directory
- Verify correct file naming (must contain patient ID)
- Make sure file correct extension (.tsv, .xlsx)

heading: 3.3.2 “Multiple files found”
text:
Cause: files type found one patientSolution:
- Remove rename duplicate files
- Make sure patient one file type
- Check BAM file patterns - might general

heading: 3.3.3 “Missing BAM or BAI”
text:
Cause: BAM file doesn’t corresponding index (.bai)Solution:
- Generate missing .bai files using samtools index
- Make sure .bai files correct naming
- file.bam must file.bam.bai file.bai

heading: 3.3.4 “Missing PDF or TSV”
text:
Cause: Incomplete Arriba outputSolution:
- Make sure files (.pdf .tsv) Arriba analysis
- Check naming - must contain string arriba correct extension (.tsv, .pdf)

heading: 3.3.5 FILL ALSO USE CASE FOR EXPRESSION FILES - MORE TISSUES THAN FILES OR MORE FILES THEN TISSUES –> TROUBLES WITH MATCHING TISSUE NAMES
